obligation_text: Testing procedures may include testing in real-world conditions. Testing in real-world conditions shall not be a condition for placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service. The testing of high-risk AI systems shall be performed at any point throughout the development process, and, in any event, prior to their being placed on the market or put into service.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system testing methodologies including real-world testing protocols, pre-market testing requirements, and integration of testing throughout the development lifecycle.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system testing methodologies including real-world testing protocols, pre-market testing requirements, and integration of testing throughout the development lifecycle.
reference_document_element_description: Design and execute AI system test and evaluation programs, producing documented test plans, results, identified failure modes, and deployment recommendations.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 9(6) (obligation) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-003 is the strongest match (STS: 0.623, cosine: 0.683). Secondary: RM-S-004 (RM, cosine: 0.634), RM-K-004 (RM, cosine: 0.628).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.623). Shared vocabulary: [ai, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [development, integration, knowledge, lifecycle, market, methodologies] vs WIDAI terms [deployment, design, documented, evaluation, execute, failure] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.886, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 21-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.683 > STS 0.623 (gap +0.060). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, system]. EU AI Act-only: [development, integration, knowledge, lifecycle, market, methodologies]. WIDAI-only: [deployment, design, documented, evaluation, execute, failure].
